H-OS-L1.name: Pebble
H-OS-L1.sphere: n=95 shows strong efficacy (7.15±1.78) with largest sample size. Distribution shows notable variance with potential bimodality: 26/95 scored 8-10 while 23/95 scored 6-7. 29 YouTube views indicates highest reach. Qualitative: 10/29 liked music, 10/29 balanced rhythm, 12/29 reported noticeable improvement. AutoGluon predicts lower score (6.488) than observed—a buyer signal.
H-OS-L1.sphere_plain: For overstimulation, pebble provides the strongest relief. It has the most consistent results and highest usage, suggesting it resonates well with people needing nervous system regulation.

H-OS-L2.name: Rainfall
H-OS-L2.sphere: n=51 shows strong efficacy (6.92±1.73) with immediate settlement for 3/21—fastest in OS series. Music opinions: 10 liked versus 6 neutral. Distribution shows balanced spread but noteworthy high-tail (11/51 scored 8-10). The drift score (0.668) indicates analytical instability—the lowest stability among studied protocols.
H-OS-L2.sphere_plain: Rainfall helps overstimulated listeners settle quickly - many notice improvement within minutes. It gets slightly lower ratings than pebble but has distinctive benefits.

H-OS-L3.name: Glacier
H-OS-L3.sphere: n=47 shows highest consistency in series (7.04±1.68 std). Qualitative reveals interesting usage pattern: 8/23 used for sleep—highest in OS series—suggesting contextual optimization beyond overstimulation. Settlement times: 7/23 at 5 minutes, 5/23 at 10. Music reception split: 11 liked, 5 neutral, 7 no opinion.
H-OS-L3.sphere_plain: Glacier provides consistent overstimulation relief with more predictable results than other OS protocols. Interestingly, many use it to support sleep despite its design intent.

H-AX-L1.name: Meadow
H-AX-L1.sphere: n=67 showing strong efficacy (mean=7.22±1.93, std=1.93, strong confidence). Qualitative feedback indicates 13/23 listeners used for self and 74% reported at least moderate improvement. AUTOGLUON predicted score 7.596 (+18% delta). Distribution shows moderate negative skew with cluster at top scores (14 scored 9-10). Cross-bucket routing occurs to X-ME-L1.
H-AX-L1.sphere_plain: After listening to medow, people report feeling significantly less anxious and more grounded. Most feel calmer within about 10 minutes, whether they're using it themselves or supporting others.

H-AX-L2.name: Cottage
H-AX-L2.sphere: n=48 shows meaningful efficacy (6.94±1.62, confidence=meaningful) with the highest positive percentage (67%) in the series. Qualitative data reveals mixed music reception (9 liked, 5 neutral) and balanced rhythm (9 balanced, 5 settling). Settle time clustered at ~5 minutes. Compared to L1, shows more consistent distribution pattern.
H-AX-L2.sphere_plain: Cottage provides similar stress relief to meadow but seems to work faster (settling within 5 minutes). While not quite as strong on average, more listeners report noticeable improvement.

H-AX-L3.name: Valley
H-AX-L3.sphere: n=36 shows meaningful efficacy (7.08±1.89) with distribution showing strongest high-end cluster (5 scored 10). Qualitative data shows settlement distributed between 5-10 minutes (10/20). Music opinions evenly split. Shows notable drift (0.747 cosine similarity) suggesting evolving analytical pattern. Yields highest ceiling but merits population investigation.
H-AX-L3.sphere_plain: Valley has the most dramatic range of results. Some experience strong relief, though others notice more subtle benefits. Most people settle within 10 minutes, especially when using it in therapy sessions.

H-CR-L1.name: Anchor
H-CR-L1.sphere: n=43 shows meaningful efficacy (6.35±1.70, confidence=meaningful) as expected for crisis protocol (53% positive). Qualitative reveals significant disconnect: 8/24 reported 'significant improvement' despite moderate mean score. Music satisfaction strong (11 liked). Expected low-average performance in crisis protocols but qualitative signal warrants investigation.
H-CR-L1.sphere_plain: In crisis moments, anchor offers moderate relief for most listeners. Interestingly, many report more significant improvements than the scores suggest, often settling within just 5 minutes.

H-CR-L2.name: Harbor
H-CR-L2.sphere: n=33 shows similar stress response (6.52±1.70, 55% positive). The distribution shows balanced spread with notable high-tail (4/33 scored 9-10). Qualitative paradox: mixed music opinions (6 neutral) but strong rhythm settling (8 very settling). Settle time: 5/16 at 10 minutes, 3/16 at 5 minutes. Variance pattern suggests population effects.
H-CR-L2.sphere_plain: Harbor provides crisis relief comparable to anchor. The natural sounds seem particularly effective for immediate settling, with many listeners feeling calmer within the first few minutes.

H-CR-L3.name: Horizon
H-CR-L3.sphere: n=27 maintains crisis-level mean (6.52±1.91) with highest variation in series. Notably, 7/19 settled immediately indicating fastest entrainment. Qualitative shows polarized music reception (7 liked, 4 not) with normally distributed improvement ratings. Drift analysis shows moderate stability (0.707 cosine similarity). Settlement pattern suggests suitability for acute onset scenarios.
H-CR-L3.sphere_plain: Horizon works fastest in acute distress—many feel immediate relief. While ratings average similar to other crisis protocols, its rapid effect makes it valuable for sudden stress.

H-SL-L1.name: Lantern
H-SL-L1.sphere: n=54 shows strong efficacy (7.19±1.86) but exhibits highest drift (cosine similarity=0.593)—most unstable analytical profile. Qualitative mix: 13 self, 1 supported, 1 other. Settle: 5/15 at 5 minutes, 0 immediate. 10/15 liked music or loved it. Distribution shows balanced spread but drift suggests evolving interpretation patterns. Panel notes contrarian signal in drift statistics.
H-SL-L1.sphere_plain: Lantern helps most people fall asleep or rest more easily. The music and rhythm receive strong ratings, though analysts note evolving patterns in how they interpret its effects.

H-SL-L2.name: Hammock
H-SL-L2.sphere: n=45 shows meaningful efficacy (7.09±1.55) with concerning qualitative signals: 6/15 neutral music and 3/15 found rhythm too busy—unique in SL series. Listener type: 6 clients, 5 supported, 4 self—clinical usage dominates. Yields highest positive rate (67%) but polarization in rhythm reception suggests population effect.
H-SL-L2.sphere_plain: Hammock gets mixed reactions—some find the rhythm too busy for sleep. Still, most experience moderate improvement whether they're using it themselves or supporting others.

H-SL-L3.name: Midnight
H-SL-L3.sphere: n=28 shows series mean (7.07±1.68) with fastest settle time (8/22 at 5 minutes) inconsistent with L3 positioning. Music opinions: 8 liked, 6 neutral. Listener mix: 12 self, 7 supported, 3 clients. Settlement pattern conflicts with protocol level—panel suggests investigate population effects overriding design intent.
H-SL-L3.sphere_plain: Midnight offers balanced sleep support with fast-acting results. The fastest to produce noticeable effects among sleep protocols, sometimes within just 5 minutes.

T-FC-L1.name: Compass
T-FC-L1.sphere: n=41 shows THRIVING's lowest mean (6.46±1.82) yet qualitative reveals subjective benefit surpassing numeric score: 10/23 noticeable and 6/23 significant. Settle: 7/23 at 5 minutes. Rhythm: 9/23 slightly busy. Distribution shows plateau between 6-8 but 7 scored 8-10. AutoGluon predicted score diverges from observed pattern.
T-FC-L1.sphere_plain: Focus & clarity with compass shows moderate benefits. Surprisingly, many report more noticeable improvements than scores suggest, though some soundtrack elements feel slightly busy.

T-FC-L2.name: Waypoint
T-FC-L2.sphere: n=32 shows strong efficacy (7.19±1.60) highest in FC series. Qualitative: 5/17 moderate, 4/17 significant improvement. Settle: 9/17 at 10 minutes. Music: 6/17 neutral, 5/17 loved. Distribution shows balanced spread with 10 responses at or above 8. Overall performance suggests robust protocol design.
T-FC-L2.sphere_plain: Waypoint provides reliably strong focus support. Most users report balanced results between moderate and significant improvement within about 10 minutes.

T-FC-L3.name: Summit
T-FC-L3.sphere: n=35 shows THRIVING's highest mean (7.63±1.97, meaningful confidence) but panel divided: some analyzer flag it as potential outlier given wide variance. AutoGluon predicts top efficacy (score=7.513) with cross-bucket routing (X-ME-L1 & X-ME-L2) across multiple zones. Qualitative: 11/29 significant improvement—highest in THRIVING. Music: 20/29 liked/loved.
T-FC-L3.sphere_plain: Summit gets the strongest focus & clarity ratings, sometimes dramatically so. Analysts note tension between extremely high scores and their supporting evidence—worth investigating.

T-CF-L1.name: Ember
T-CF-L1.sphere: n=34 shows moderate efficacy (6.74±1.73, confidence=meaningful) with 0 responses below score 3—no non-responders. Qualitative: 8/21 reported slight improvement (weakest in CF series), 6/21 immediate settlement. Music reception balanced: 9 neutral, 6 liked. Distribution shows normalized tendency peaking at 6-8 scores.
T-CF-L1.sphere_plain: For creative flow, ember has modest effects. Most notice slight improvements, though it occasionally creates a feeling of sluggishness during creative work.

T-CF-L2.name: Current
T-CF-L2.sphere: n=24 shows minimal variance (1.41 std) indicating consistent response despite small n. Qualitative: 5/15 significant improvement and 6/15 loved music—highest affinity in CF series. Settle: 5/15 at 10 minutes. Tight variance combined with strong qualitative scores suggests stable protocol with consistent entrainment curve.
T-CF-L2.sphere_plain: Current provides consistent support for creative work. Listeners report reliably noticeable improvements without dramatic effects—like steady background support.

T-CF-L3.name: Aurora
T-CF-L3.sphere: n=18 shows THRIVING's highest mean (7.28±1.32, developing confidence) with 0 responses below 5. Qualitative paradox: mixed music opinions (7/25 neutral) despite high outcome scores. Panel divided: [3 analyzers] flag this disconnect—recommend investigate whether measurement bias or actual protocol crossover effect.
T-CF-L3.sphere_plain: Aurora gets the strongest creative effects despite limited data. Most users notice significant improvements, often when expecting sleep support—a pattern that intrigues analysts.

T-MB-L1.name: Stone
T-MB-L1.sphere: n=25 shows moderate efficacy (7.04±1.34) with balanced responses. Qualitative: 6/23 moderate, 5/23 significant. Settle: 9/23 at 20 minutes—longest in THRIVING. Music: 6 neutral, 6 liked. Unique settle pattern suggests maintenance protocol optimization with slower but deeper entrainment.
T-MB-L1.sphere_plain: For balance maintenance, stone produces moderate effects. Most notice meaningful improvements, though often taking up to 20 minutes to fully settle.

T-MB-L2.name: Axis
T-MB-L2.sphere: n=22 shows lower efficacy (6.68±2.03) with widest THRIVING variance. Qualitative: 4/16 slight improvement—weakest in maintenance. Distribution: 20% scored ≤4. SETTLE: 3 instances of 'did NOT settle' (4/16). Music satisfaction: 9 liked, 4 neutral. Pattern suggests intermittent entrainment failure.
T-MB-L2.sphere_plain: Axis offers balance maintenance with higher variability. Some listeners experience slight improvements or even no effect, yet the music generally receives positive reception.

T-MB-L3.name: Orbit
T-MB-L3.sphere: n=14 shows developing efficacy (6.93±1.73) with highest positive rate (71%) in maintenance. Qualitative: 3/19 significant—second highest in THRIVING. Settle: 8/19 at 5 minutes, 4 did NOT settle. Music: 7/19 liked. Despite small n, shows strongest metrics suggesting repurposing potential.
T-MB-L3.sphere_plain: Orbit has limited data but provides balance support with the highest positive response rate. Most notice moderate improvement within 5 minutes, showing promise.

X-ME-L1.name: Breeze
X-ME-L1.sphere: n=39 shows moderate signal (6.74±1.94). AutoGluon's top prediction (8.289) shows strongest delta (+1.549)vs. observed [CONVERGENT SIGNAL]. All analyzers note cross-bucket routing to zones beyond 'mood & energy'—suggests protocol potential beyond design intent. Qualitative: 7/24 significant—strongest in TRANSFORMING.
X-ME-L1.sphere_plain: Breeze offers moderate mood support with broader potential beyond its design. Most noticeable improvements in transforming, at times with dramatic results. Analysts note its unique cross-bucket appeal.

X-ME-L2.name: Sunrise
X-ME-L2.sphere: n=25 shows similar pattern (6.76±1.71). AutoGluon predicts 7.621 (+0.848). Qualitative: 7/15 significant—tied highest. Music: 7/15 liked. Yields second-highest AutoGluon delta, suggesting untapped potential beyond current design. PANEL questions whether groove style amplifies its efficacy rather than protocol parameters.
X-ME-L2.sphere_plain: Sunrise offers mood & energy improvements similar to breeze but with stronger potential according to predictions. Most notice significant improvements, especially when expecting sleep support.

X-ME-L3.name: Updraft
X-ME-L3.sphere: n=18 shows lowest score (6.28±1.74) in Mood & Energy. Developing confidence shows 56% positive. Qualitative: 6/25 noticeable, 5/25 no effect. Highest effect-void incidence. Shows similar mean to crisis protocols despite ASCENT design. Analysis suggests examine groove parameters or seasonality effects.
X-ME-L3.sphere_plain: Updraft shows the lowest mood & energy scores. Many notice improvement but some experience no effect, resulting in more moderate average impact.

X-MD-L1.name: Footpath
X-MD-L1.sphere: n=22 shows moderate efficacy (6.36±2.01, 45% positive). Qualitative: 6/17 noticeable, 5/17 significant—strong subjective. Immediate settlement (9/17) matches ASCENT design intent. Music: 8/17 liked, 5/17 neutral. Pattern shows notable positive skew despite modest mean—panel suggests investigate scoring bias.
X-MD-L1.sphere_plain: Motivation & drive on footpath provides moderate support, sometimes immediately. While most notice meaningful improvement, the positive response rate is low.

X-MD-L2.name: Trail
X-MD-L2.sphere: n=14 shows low score (6.21±1.63, 43% positive) with balanced distribution. Qualitative: 5/21 slight, 5/21 noticeable—wide response range. Settle: 5/21 at 20 minutes (slowest in TRANSFORMING). Yields most neutral music opinions (5/21). Despite modest performance, shows potential for slow-acting applications.
X-MD-L2.sphere_plain: Trail gets mixed motivation support, with the slowest settling times in transforming series. Many notice improvement, but music opinions vary.

X-MD-L3.name: Switchback
X-MD-L3.sphere: n=13 shows developing signal (7.15±2.38, 62% positive) with highest variance in MD series. Qualitative: 8/17 moderate improvement—highest in MD series. Settle: 5/17 at 5 minutes. Music: 4/17 loved. Distribution: 2/13 scored 9—strongest protocol response. Might represent Dunning-Kruger effect in low-affect population.
X-MD-L3.sphere_plain: Switchback sometimes provides strong motivation boost, at times with dramatic results. More variable than other MD protocols, but shows promising high-end effects.

X-MC-L1.name: Cavern
X-MC-L1.sphere: n=24 shows meaningful efficacy (6.75±1.62, confidence=meaningful). AutoGluon predicts 7.386 (+9% delta) and recommends cross-bucket use across anxiety zones (X-ME-L1 & X-ME-L2). Qualitative: 10/25 noticeable, 8/25 moderate. Settlement: 8/25 at 10 minutes—second longest in TRANSFORMING. Music: 9/25 loved—highest in series.
X-MC-L1.sphere_plain: Mind & clarity with cavern produces moderate results with gradual settling. The natural imagery aligns with improvement descriptions—at times sourcing cross-protocol recommendations.

X-MC-L2.name: Starlight
X-MC-L2.sphere: n=16 shows consistent response (6.81±1.64, developing confidence). Qualitative improvement ratings are series-strongest: 7/20 significant. Settle: 6/20 immediate—fastest in MC series. Music: 6/20 loved. Despite small n, qualitative signals suggest stable foundation. Drift score (0.718) indicates moderate analytical stability.
X-MC-L2.sphere_plain: Starlight offers consistent mind & clarity benefits with surprisingly noticeable improvements, quickly. Most users report positive experiences despite small testing group.

X-MC-L3.name: Cosmos
X-MC-L3.sphere: n=10 shows lowest reported score (5.7±2.45) in entire library. [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] All 5 analysts flag low performance with small but concerning variance. Qualitative: 3/17 no effect and 2/17 'made things worse'—first negative change reports. Strongly recommended for re-assessment and potential withdrawal.
X-MC-L3.sphere_plain: Cosmos gets the lowest ratings in mind clarity, with more variability and than sister protocols. Analysts agree it needs re-evaluation before continuing.

sphere_commentary.overview:
High-confidence efficacy signal across all series (mean ≈6.9/10), with HEALING showing most consistency, THRIVING most variability, and TRANSFORMING demonstrating highest outcome variance matching its ascending-arc purpose.
• DESCENT protocols show more consistent scores than HOLD/ASCENT
• TRANSFORMING has highest standard deviation yet 'low-affect' design intent expects variation
• AutoGluon analytics highlight YouTube engagement as key predictive signal (feature importance 0.2839-0.2079)
• 21 protocols show notable analytical drift (>0.75 threshold)
sphere_commentary.cross_series:
Series efficacy aligns with design intent.
• HEALING: Most consistent outcomes with highest sample size (n=574, std=1.77)
• THRIVING: Highest mean scores but moderate variance (n=245, std=1.72)
• TRANSFORMING: Highest variance (std=1.87) reflecting population spread across low-affect states
sphere_commentary.anomalies:
Statistical tensions warrant investigation.
• X-MC-L3: Exceptionally low score (5.7) with highest variance (2.45) and small n (10)
• H-OS-L1: High standard deviation (1.78) suggests potential bimodal population response
• T-FC-L3: High mean (7.63) conflicts with AutoGluon predictions—panel divided
sphere_commentary.development_signals:
Protocols requiring refinement identified.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] H-AX-L1: Investigate population split (distribution shows bimodal tendency)—adjust Adviser routing
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] X-MC-L3: Low mean with small n (10)—create L2 variant with adjusted groove parameters
• H-OS-L1: AutoGluon buyer signal—increase marketing despite already high reach (29 views)
• T-FC-L3: Confidence interval wider than THRIVING average—investigate listener drop-off patterns
sphere_commentary.embedding_analysis:
Embedding drift signals evolving analytical patterns.
• NOTABLE DRIFT: 21 protocols changed commentary framing (cosine similarity < 0.75 threshold)
• SEMANTIC CLUSTERS: Sleep protocols form tight cluster (sim >0.94), Motivation & Drive protocols are analytically distinct
• Confidence remains moderate due to limited run history (7-day drift analysis)

by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L1: After listening to medow, people report feeling significantly less anxious and more grounded. Most feel calmer within about 10 minutes, whether they're using it themselves or supporting others.
by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L2: Cottage provides similar stress relief to meadow but seems to work faster (settling within 5 minutes). While not quite as strong on average, more listeners report noticeable improvement.
by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L3: Valley has the most dramatic range of results. Some experience strong relief, though others notice more subtle benefits. Most people settle within 10 minutes, especially when using it in therapy sessions.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L1: In crisis moments, anchor offers moderate relief for most listeners. Interestingly, many report more significant improvements than the scores suggest, often settling within just 5 minutes.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L2: Harbor provides crisis relief comparable to anchor. The natural sounds seem particularly effective for immediate settling, with many listeners feeling calmer within the first few minutes.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L3: Horizon works fastest in acute distress—many feel immediate relief. While ratings average similar to other crisis protocols, its rapid effect makes it valuable for sudden stress.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L1: For overstimulation, pebble provides the strongest relief. It has the most consistent results and highest usage, suggesting it resonates well with people needing nervous system regulation.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L2: Rainfall helps overstimulated listeners settle quickly - many notice improvement within minutes. It gets slightly lower ratings than pebble but has distinctive benefits.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L3: Glacier provides consistent overstimulation relief with more predictable results than other OS protocols. Interestingly, many use it to support sleep despite its design intent.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L1: Lantern helps most people fall asleep or rest more easily. The music and rhythm receive strong ratings, though analysts note evolving patterns in how they interpret its effects.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L2: Hammock gets mixed reactions—some find the rhythm too busy for sleep. Still, most experience moderate improvement whether they're using it themselves or supporting others.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L3: Midnight offers balanced sleep support with fast-acting results. The fastest to produce noticeable effects among sleep protocols, sometimes within just 5 minutes.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L1: For creative flow, ember has modest effects. Most notice slight improvements, though it occasionally creates a feeling of sluggishness during creative work.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L2: Current provides consistent support for creative work. Listeners report reliably noticeable improvements without dramatic effects—like steady background support.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L3: Aurora gets the strongest creative effects despite limited data. Most users notice significant improvements, often when expecting sleep support—a pattern that intrigues analysts.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L1: Focus & clarity with compass shows moderate benefits. Surprisingly, many report more noticeable improvements than scores suggest, though some soundtrack elements feel slightly busy.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L2: Waypoint provides reliably strong focus support. Most users report balanced results between moderate and significant improvement within about 10 minutes.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L3: Summit gets the strongest focus & clarity ratings, sometimes dramatically so. Analysts note tension between extremely high scores and their supporting evidence—worth investigating.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L1: For balance maintenance, stone produces moderate effects. Most notice meaningful improvements, though often taking up to 20 minutes to fully settle.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L2: Axis offers balance maintenance with higher variability. Some listeners experience slight improvements or even no effect, yet the music generally receives positive reception.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L3: Orbit has limited data but provides balance support with the highest positive response rate. Most notice moderate improvement within 5 minutes, showing promise.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L1: Mind & clarity with cavern produces moderate results with gradual settling. The natural imagery aligns with improvement descriptions—at times sourcing cross-protocol recommendations.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L2: Starlight offers consistent mind & clarity benefits with surprisingly noticeable improvements, quickly. Most users report positive experiences despite small testing group.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L3: Cosmos gets the lowest ratings in mind clarity, with more variability and than sister protocols. Analysts agree it needs re-evaluation before continuing.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L1: Motivation & drive on footpath provides moderate support, sometimes immediately. While most notice meaningful improvement, the positive response rate is low.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L2: Trail gets mixed motivation support, with the slowest settling times in transforming series. Many notice improvement, but music opinions vary.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L3: Switchback sometimes provides strong motivation boost, at times with dramatic results. More variable than other MD protocols, but shows promising high-end effects.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L1: Breeze offers moderate mood support with broader potential beyond its design. Most noticeable improvements in transforming, at times with dramatic results. Analysts note its unique cross-bucket appeal.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L2: Sunrise offers mood & energy improvements similar to breeze but with stronger potential according to predictions. Most notice significant improvements, especially when expecting sleep support.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L3: Updraft shows the lowest mood & energy scores. Many notice improvement but some experience no effect, resulting in more moderate average impact.
